title: Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus
what_description: Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus is a plant species within the family Fabaceae, native to North America. It is a perennial herb with a taproot, growing to 20-60 cm in height, and has pubescent stems and leaflets. The leaflets are ovate to elliptic in shape, 2-5 cm in length, and have a cordate base. The flowers are pink or purple, 6-9 mm in length, and are arranged in axillary racemes. The fruit is a pod, 6-10 mm in length, containing 2-4 seeds.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus are inflammation and fatigue, which are reduced by its anti-inflammatory and adaptogenic properties. It also helps manage diabetes through its ability to regulate blood sugar levels and improve insulin sensitivity. Additionally, it has been shown to lower hypertension in individuals by reducing blood pressure and improving cardiovascular health. Furthermore, it supports immune system function by increasing antibody production and activating natural killer cells, and it has been used to treat anemia by increasing red blood cell count and hemoglobin levels. Its antioxidant properties also make it effective in treating digestive issues, cancer, arthritis, asthma, gastroenteritis, gastrointestinal disorders, cardiovascular disease, skin conditions, chronic fatigue, and respiratory infections.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus are its antioxidant activity, which is primarily attributed to the flavonoids present in the plant. Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which are mainly due to the saponins in the plant, that help reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. The immunomodulatory effects of Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus are largely attributed to the volatile oils, which have been shown to modulate the immune system and prevent infections. Additionally, the plant's antimicrobial properties, which are mainly due to the presence of alkaloids, prevent the growth of microorganisms and treat infections. Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus also exhibits cardioprotective properties, which are primarily attributed to the flavonoids and saponins in the plant, that help prevent cardiovascular diseases by reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus are astragaloside IV, astragaloside III, astragaloside VI, saponins, and astragaloside VIII. These constituents are responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which are attributed to their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and nitric oxide, while also exhibiting scavenging activity against free radicals. Astragaloside IV has been shown to possess cardioprotective and neuroprotective effects, while astragaloside III has been found to have hepatoprotective properties. Astragaloside VI and astragaloside VIII have been reported to exhibit immunomodulatory and anti-inflammatory activities, and saponins have been shown to possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The combination of these constituents contributes to the plant's overall therapeutic potential in treating various diseases, including cardiovascular, neurological, and inflammatory disorders.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus are astragalus tea, astragalus tincture, astragalus infusion, decoction, and astragalus capsules. Astragalus tea is made by infusing the roots and stems in hot water to release the bioactive compounds. Astragalus tincture is a concentrated liquid extract made by soaking the plant material in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin. Astragalus infusion is similar to tea but made with cold water and a longer steep time to extract more of the plant's constituents. Astragalus decoction is made by boiling the plant material in water to extract the soluble compounds, while astragalus capsules are filled with dried and powdered plant material for convenience and standardized dosing.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus improperly include interacting with medications such as blood thinners, diuretics, and diabetes medications by enhancing their effects or reducing their efficacy, which can lead to adverse reactions. Additionally, Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus can induce nausea, vomiting, and stomach discomfort in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large doses or without proper preparation. Furthermore, Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus may exacerbate kidney disease by increasing the risk of kidney damage or worsening existing kidney function, as it can act as a diuretic and increase urine production. Consuming Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus can also cause vomiting, especially when taken with other medications or herbs that have a similar effect on the digestive system. Moreover, Astragalus exscapus subsp. pubiflorus may increase blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, as it can stimulate the cardiovascular system and lead to an increase in heart rate and blood pressure.
